title: Net Present Value of Growth Opportunities (NPVGO)
summary: Calculates the value added to a firm from future growth opportunities, such as new projects or acquisitions. It assesses the intrinsic value per share generated by these growth initiatives by discounting future cash flows and subtracting initial investment.
applicable_when: Evaluating new projects, acquisitions, or expansion plans.
Assessing the value derived from a company's future growth initiatives.
Determining the intrinsic value per share attributed to growth.
Comparing the profitability of various growth opportunities.
Performing equity valuation to separate value from assets-in-place and growth opportunities.
not_applicable_when: Analyzing the value of assets-in-place without considering future growth.
When a firm has no identifiable growth opportunities.
When projected cash inflows from growth are not available.
When the initial investment for growth opportunities is unknown.
When the cost of capital for discounting is not provided or is zero/negative.

common_confusions.label: Net Present Value (NPV)
common_confusions.difference: NPV measures the profitability of a specific project or investment over its entire life, including initial investment and all future cash flows. NPVGO specifically isolates the value added by growth opportunities, often as a component of the overall equity value, not the total project value.

common_confusions.label: Enterprise Value (EV)
common_confusions.difference: EV represents the total value of a company, including both equity and debt, and takes into account assets-in-place and growth. NPVGO focuses solely on the value contribution from growth opportunities to equity holders, not the entire firm's value.

common_confusions.label: Price-to-Earnings (P/E) Ratio
common_confusions.difference: P/E is a valuation multiple that compares a company's current share price to its earnings per share. NPVGO is an absolute dollar value metric that specifically quantifies the value of growth opportunities, rather than a relative market multiple.
